themes.label: AI Hardware Supercycle
themes.narrative: The AI capex bubble is deflating. Post-AVGO earnings, stocks sold off on a minor guidance sub-line miss. The infrastructure spend is peaking. Hyperscalers will cut capex.
themes.dataAnchor: AVGO Q2 2026 (June 3): revenue $22.2B (+48% YoY, beat estimate of $22.04B). AI revenue $10.8B (+143% YoY). Q3 guided $29.4B total (+84% YoY), AI revenue guided >200% YoY. Order book confirmed >$70B. The stock fell -3% post-earnings on a minor infrastructure software miss — not on AI hardware. That is the data-narrative gap.
themes.counterRead: The market read the minor software miss as a capex-peak signal. The AI hardware data disagrees: $10.8B AI revenue in Q2 followed by >200% guided growth in Q3 is not a plateau. The custom ASIC order book of >$70B from Google, Meta, and Apple represents years of revenue already contracted. The -3% dip is mispricing. The entry window is open now before Q3 results confirm the acceleration.
themes.watchFor: AVGO Q3 results (Sept 2026): AI revenue confirm >$12B quarter. Any hyperscaler capex cut in quarterly guidance would change this read.

themes.label: Defense / Nuclear Backlog
themes.narrative: Nuclear stocks are speculative SMR plays. Government defense stocks are interest-rate sensitive and tied to discretionary budget decisions.
themes.dataAnchor: BWXT Q1 2026: $860M revenue, FY guided $3.75B+. Backlog $8.65B (+77% YoY) as of March 2026. $1.29B long-lead US Naval Nuclear Propulsion award + $165M Ford-class aircraft carrier reactor contract. US naval nuclear reactor production is mandated by federal law — not discretionary. 60% of remaining backlog converts to revenue by end of 2027. Deutsche Bank upgraded. Zero AI disruption risk.
themes.counterRead: BWXT is not an SMR speculation — it is a legally mandated defense supplier with $8.65B in confirmed, contracted backlog. The narrative lumps it with speculative nuclear names like OKLO. The data shows 2.3x annual revenue in backlog that was already signed before today. The market is pricing it as a cyclical industrial; it behaves more like a utility with contracted revenue visibility.
themes.watchFor: US defense budget sequestration cut >20% (would reduce naval nuclear orders). SMR commercial order flow would be upside catalyst, not base case.

themes.label: SaaSpocalypse — who survives
themes.narrative: Agentic AI (January 2026 breakout) is destroying the seat-based SaaS revenue model. IGV software ETF -21% YTD. All enterprise software is going to zero. AI agents replace every CRM seat, every monitoring tool, every workflow.
themes.dataAnchor: The macro is real: IGV -21% YTD, -30% from September 2025 peak. BUT the narrative is painting every software company with the same brush. DDOG Q1 2026: +32% YoY to $1.01B (first $1B quarter), FCF $289M at 29% margin, four consecutive quarters of accelerating growth — raised guide $240M. AI generates MORE logs and metrics, not fewer. HUBS Q1 +23% but management itself warned on AI transition to seat-based CRM — that risk is real and self-disclosed. NOW Q1 +22%, $1.67B FCF, 44% margin — workflow automation embedded too deeply to displace quickly.
themes.counterRead: The blanket SaaS sell-off creates separation between beneficiaries and casualties. DDOG benefits from AI proliferation (more AI workloads = more observability data). HUBS is genuinely at risk (seat-based CRM is what AI agents cannibalize first). The opportunity is in companies where AI is additive to revenue, not destructive. DDOG's accelerating growth while its sector declines is the signal.
themes.watchFor: HUBS Q2 2026: whether seat expansion stabilizes or accelerates the decline. DDOG Q2 2026: whether the $1B+ quarterly revenue pace holds and guide raises again.

themes.label: Industrial / Infrastructure Rotation
themes.narrative: Industrials are expensive after a multi-year run. The easy money in power infrastructure has been made. VRT has had its moment.
themes.dataAnchor: VRT Q4 2025 orders: +252% YoY. FCF $1.9B. $15B order book. FY2026 organic growth guided 27-29%. RSI at 36.2. Down -14.8% last month. 22 of 26 covering banks rate Buy or Strong Buy. The macro rotation INTO industrials/energy/materials from tech/SaaS is active: Energy and Industrials are the YTD leaders while IGV -21%. The VRT dislocation vs. the industrial macro trend is the gap.
themes.counterRead: VRT at 49x forward P/E carries real downside (-30-40%) if hyperscaler capex decelerates. The bear case is not wrong — it just requires a specific scenario. Against that: the $15B order book provides 18+ months of revenue visibility that doesn't evaporate in one quarter. The industrial macro rotation is institutional and persistent. The risk-reward improves the further it corrects.
themes.watchFor: VRT Q2 2026: order rate and margin. Any two consecutive quarters of hyperscaler capex guidance cuts would validate the bear case.

themes.label: Credit / Macro Posture
themes.narrative: Recession is coming. Rate hikes broke credit. Consumer is weakening.
themes.dataAnchor: HY OAS: 2.75% — historically tight. Not pricing credit stress. Yield curve: 2s10s steepening (+41bp) — historically a recovery signal, not a recession signal. Fed at 3.50-3.75% with cuts in progress. The Kostolany Phase D read remains intact: rates down, credit available, economy stabilizing. SPY above 200d MA.
themes.counterRead: No significant divergence between the macro narrative and the credit data. HY spreads widen before recessions — they are tightening. This is a multiple compression event in growth stocks (duration sensitivity to real rates), not a credit event. The posture stays: Hold and sharpen. Preserve capital in defensible names, sharpen positions in verified dislocations.
themes.watchFor: HY OAS above 4.0% — credit starting to price stress, posture shifts defensive. SPY breaks 200d MA — structural breakdown, re-evaluate all positions.

themes.label: Aviation Supercycle — Backlog Reality
themes.narrative: Commercial aviation recovery is complete. Airlines recovered from COVID. The easy money in aerospace is made. Industrial supercycles end when everyone knows about them.
themes.dataAnchor: GE Aerospace FY2026: $211B backlog that grows every quarter. LEAP engine delivery queue exceeds manufacturing capacity — airlines CANNOT receive planes fast enough. Q1 2026: revenue +25% YoY, $8-8.4B FCF guided FY2026. Narrow-body delivery lead times extended to 2028+. Defense engine programs (F-35, military) provide parallel, mandated revenue. This is not speculative: airlines have paid deposits on planes that require GE engines.
themes.counterRead: The narrative conflates 'COVID recovery complete' with 'aerospace opportunity done' — two different claims. The new structural driver is fleet renewal: the global fleet is aging, fuel efficiency mandates require new engine technology, and aviation demand in emerging markets is growing structurally. The LEAP backlog at $211B and growing while the narrative says 'industrials already ran' is the DATA_AHEAD gap. Best entry at ~35x forward PE or on an earnings-driven dislocation.
themes.watchFor: GE Q2 2026 backlog total — any decline signals demand saturation. Boeing production rate — Boeing bottlenecks constrain GE engine deliveries; acceleration is positive catalyst. Defense budget cuts to military engine programs.

themes.label: Midstream Pipelines as AI Infrastructure Income
themes.narrative: Pipeline companies are boring commodity stocks with too much debt and ESG risk. Energy is cyclical. AI investment means semiconductors and cloud — not natural gas pipelines.
themes.dataAnchor: Energy Transfer (ET): $25B in firm-fee AI data center gas contracts (Oracle signed, Nexus Hubbard AI campus contracted) at 18-year average duration. Fee payments are contractual — not contingent on gas prices, contingent on volumes flowing through pipes AI data centers have already committed to use. Q1 2026 DCF +17%. 7-8% LP distribution yield. AI data centers are the largest new US electricity demand source — that electricity is primarily gas-fired — gas travels by pipeline. The causal chain is direct.
themes.counterRead: The market classifies ET as an energy stock. Correct classification: AI infrastructure income vehicle with contractual fee structure. $25B in contracted fees over 18 years is not commodity exposure — it is infrastructure annuity income with an AI demand driver. The narrative says 'pipelines are boring energy stocks.' The data says ET has sold 18 years of AI infrastructure pipeline capacity at contractual rates. The misclassification is the opportunity. 7-8% yield provides income while waiting for narrative convergence.
themes.watchFor: ET Q2 2026 DCF must confirm AI data center gas volume ramp — any QoQ decline changes the thesis. Hyperscaler capex guidance cuts >20% reduce gas demand growth. Any adverse MLP tax treatment regulatory change.

strategyPosture: Get in early — before the narrative catches up to the data. The framework: identify where DATA is AHEAD of narrative, find names before institutional crowding, enter at dislocations with clear catalysts and defined invalidation. Current top conviction early-entry positions: BWXT (legal monopoly priced as cyclical — $8.65B backlog is pre-sold), CLS (undiscovered AI hardware at 18x PE — same demand as AVGO at 32x), ET (AI infrastructure income misclassified as energy stock — $25B in contracts), KNSL (Buffett-template quality compounder down 24% for narrative reasons). The crowd has not arrived in any of these names. This is the window. Capital preservation filter stays strict: FCF positive, no binary risk, clear invalidation defined. No defensive action required — credit clean (HY OAS 2.75%), Phase D intact.
whereWaveBuilds: CLS: same AI hardware demand as AVGO at a fraction of the multiple, no institutional crowding (pre-consensus). ET: $25B in contracted AI infrastructure fees at 7-8% yield while waiting for narrative recognition. KNSL: 77.4% combined ratio compounder down 24% — narrative mismatch, not fundamental deterioration. BWXT: legal monopoly backlog converting through 2027 — legal mandate makes it utility-like, not cyclical. AVGO: $29.4B Q3 guide already confirmed, post-dip entry window narrowing as institutions recognize. The wave does NOT build in: momentum-chased names (MU +70% YTD, UBER +40% YTD), geopolitical binary risk (TSM), not-FCF-positive growth (NTRA), or seat-based SaaS (HUBS).
watchFor: CLS Q2 2026: FY guide raised above $19B — confirms AI hardware manufacturing thesis and forces institutional recognition
KNSL Q2 2026: combined ratio below 80% — confirms underwriting quality intact through E&S softening narrative
ET Q2 2026: DCF confirms AI data center gas volume ramp — validates midstream-as-AI-infrastructure thesis
AVGO Q3 Sept 2026: AI revenue confirm >$12B — validates $29.4B guide and post-Q2 entry thesis
BWXT additional naval contract awards — accelerates backlog above $10B threshold
DDOG Q2 2026: revenue above $1B with raised guide — confirms AI-monitoring thesis while sector rotates
HY OAS above 4.0% — posture shifts to capital preservation mode immediately, all positions re-evaluated
Any two consecutive hyperscaler capex guidance cuts — invalidates VRT, AVGO, CLS, ET infrastructure theses simultaneously
